hypothesis_en: Mr President, the objective of this directive, which is the welfare of the transport of animals in decent conditions, deserves our full support, but we want all the decisions we take in this respect to be based on scientific and technical criteria. We cannot legislate behind the back of an economic reality, because there is economic activity around the transport of animals in many areas of the European Union. The Commission has maintained from the outset its desire to adjust the travel times allowed to social norms, but the truth is, Commissioner, that the best thing for animal welfare is to reach its destination as soon as possible. I would like to ask the Commissioner whether he is aware of the fact that the Commission has already made a number of proposals to the Council and Parliament on this subject, and whether he is aware of the fact that the Council has already made a number of proposals to the Council. In order to guarantee the dignity of the transport of animals, the focus must be on the good conditions of the vehicles carrying them, on the good preparation of the transport staff and, above all, on ensuring that the rules we decide here are respected throughout the European Union.
reference_en: thank you President (2.41) the purpose of this Directive namely the welfare (1.37) of animals ensuring that they can be transported in decent conditions deserves our full backing but we feel that all the decisions we take on this (1.24) are based on scientific and technical criteria at the same time we we shouldn't overlook the point that animal transport is subject to the rules of the internal market (1.05) we are not able to legislate (1.07) while turning our backs on economic realities (1.87) because about and on animal transport there's an area of economic ac= activity here for wide centres of the European Union so I can't agree with the rapporteur (1.48) regarding the difference between animals (1.06) intended for slaughter and live transport the need for d= decent conditions for transport applies to both categories of animals when it comes to the hours (1.06) involved in transport right from the beginning the Commission has shown there's a desire to align the the periods with the requirements of social protection for drivers but the the the main thing is to get the animals to the destination as quickly as possible the twelve-hour limit (1.22) clearly would have a harmful impact on animals transport (1.82) but nine hours (1.35) clearly would attack the principle of the single market and so we feel that (1.2) a cons= consistent proper proposal would be to make it compulsory to have two drivers doing nine-hour shifts so that they could have short breaks and then continue another nine-hour section of transport lastly may I say that we feel that all our efforts on this proposal (1.14) to ensure decent conditions for transport of animals (2.21) must revolve around good conditions of the vehicles they're transported in there must be proper preparation proper training of the the staff involved in transporting them and above all we must work to ensure that the rules we decide on are enforced right across the European Union thank you
source_es: gracias señor Presidente el el objetivo de esta Directiva que es e= el bienestar e= en el transporte de los animales en condiciones dignas merece todo nuestro apoyo pero queremos que todas las decisiones que adoptemos a este respecto estén basadas en criterios científicos y técnicos por otra parte no podemos olvidar que el transporte de animales no escapa a las reglas y a las normas del mercado interior y que no podemos legislar de espaldas a una realidad económica porque alrededor del transporte de los animales existe una actividad económica para muchas zonas de la Unión Europea no podemos compartir la propuesta del ponente en relación a la diferencia entre animales para sacrificio y animales para vida la dignidad en el transporte afecta a ambos animales por igual en relación a las horas de transporte la Comisión ha mantenido desde el principio su deseo de ajustar las horas de viaje permitidas a la normativa social pero lo cierto señor Comisario es que lo mejor para el bienestar animal es llegar cuanto antes a su lugar de destino el descanso de doce horas de los animales en el vehículo atenta clarísimamente a la al bienestar y a la dignidad de los animales en el transporte pero la limitación de nueve horas en el transporte atenta clarísimamente también al principio del mercado interior por eso nosotros pensamos que una solución o una propuesta coherente correcta sería establecer la obligación de dos conductores con un ciclo de nueve horas que permitiera un descanso breve para poder continuar otro ciclo de nueve horas en el transporte por último decir que nos parece que todo nuestro esfuerzo en esta propuesta para garantizar la dignidad en el transporte de los animales se debe centrar en una bu= en unas buenas condiciones de los vehículos que les transportan en una buena preparación de lo az= del personal encargado del transporte y sobre todo sobre todo en hacer que las normas que aquí decidamos se cumplan en todo el territorio de la Unión Europea muchas gracias
